title: Constraint conservation transfer

queries: graph contrastive learning preserve node identity across transformed views
graph prompt alignment preserve structure during graph-to-text transfer
knowledge graph alignment invariant preservation sparse basis assignment

papers.title: GraphCL: Contrastive Self-Supervised Learning of Graph Representations
papers.claim: Contrastive agreement across transformed graph views preserves transferable identity signal.
papers.relevance: Direct paper basis for preserving seed-entity identity before and after basis-aligned transfer.

papers.title: GraphMAE: Self-Supervised Masked Graph Autoencoders
papers.claim: Better graph-side representations make downstream transfer more stable.
papers.relevance: Part 3 is explicitly built on top of the validated Part 1 artifact-backed GNN representation path.

papers.title: Heterogeneous Graph Masked Autoencoders
papers.claim: Heterogeneous graph representation quality benefits from structure-aware constraints.
papers.relevance: Supports preserving schema/value structural cues during heterogeneous graph-to-token transfer.

evidence.why_now: EXP192-family Phase 2 established that the current Part 1 artifact-backed base is scientifically meaningful but mixed: user-ltv and item-incoterms beat full-test reference while user-churn stayed below reference. The next justified step is therefore not another identical rerun, but the documented Part 3 constraint-conservation layer on top of the validated Part 1 base, using the same task-specific best settings for the first fair comparison.

hypothesis: If the validated Part 1 path is still losing useful relational signal because basis transfer over-mixes schema and value evidence during graph-to-token projection, then activating the documented constraint-conservation transfer layer under the same task-specific best hyperparameters should preserve more identity and structural evidence without changing the upstream GNN artifact or conflating the result with a new tuning sweep.
notes: This is the first fair comparison candidate after turning the documented Part 3 constraint-conservation knobs into a real code path.
Do not run Optuna or final full test from this candidate directly.
The first comparison wave must keep the current task-specific Phase 2 best settings fixed so the comparison is mechanism-level rather than tuning-level.
Because batch_size is per-rank, later launch preparation should preserve the intended per-rank batch semantics when comparing against the prior 2-rank best-setting runs.

Scientific interpretation still follows the same Stage 3 rules: subset metrics are continuation gates, user-ltv subset/full-test scale mismatch still applies, and item-incoterms should still be judged scientifically against the full-test reference band near 0.70 rather than the optimistic subset regime.
